items.title: 'Bio-stickers' speed up plastic breakdown in marine environments
items.summary: Plastic waste poses an urgent problem for the planet's ecosystems, especially in waterways. Millions of tons of plastic waste enter Earth's oceans every year, and plastic has been found in every part of the ocean, including at the bottom of the deepest ocean trenches.

items.title: Research investigation shows 'bossware' is spying on workers and sharing their data
items.summary: A new investigation finds that workplace monitoring platforms are systematically sharing personal data about workers and online activity with hundreds of outside data brokers and big tech companies in ways that are not clearly disclosed and that, in some cases, may contradict the platforms' own

items.title: Indonesians mark 20 years since mud volcano eruption swallowed up entire communities in East Java
items.summary: Residents in the East Java province of Indonesia scattered flowers, paid their respects and prayed at the edge of a mud lake on Friday, the 20th anniversary of the eruption of the Lusi mud volcano that inundated villages and killed at least 14 people.

items.title: Megafire kills Joshua trees, but not fungi
items.summary: When the Dome Fire tore through the Mojave Desert in 2020, it reduced 1 million Eastern Joshua trees to blackened skeletons. Scientists expected the underground ecosystem to be equally devastated. Instead, they found it thriving.

items.title: Wattle's the deal with psychedelics?
items.summary: In 2008, while investigating a clandestine drug lab, forensic scientists from WA's ChemCentre found something odd—a pile of wet bark, stripped from a wattle tree and stewed.

items.title: Forever chemical reaches fish before they even hatch, new study reveals
items.summary: There is a forever chemical lurking in the world's oceans that could be fundamentally altering the biology of marine life before it even hatches. PFOS, a notorious member of the PFAS family of chemicals, is known for its ability to bioaccumulate, binding specifically to proteins in the blood an

items.title: Routine questionnaire could help thousands of children thrive at school, study finds
items.summary: A routine questionnaire completed by parents when their child turns two could play a vital role in identifying children who need extra support before they start primary school, a new study has revealed.

items.title: A climate fix with a hidden catch: Cutting methane reshapes ozone layer's comeback in unexpected ways
items.summary: Reducing methane emissions will slow climate change but could also slow the recovery of the stratospheric ozone layer, new research from the University of Reading shows.

items.title: 'Shoot for the moon?' Aim a bit lower, researchers say
items.summary: How ambitious should you be? Folk wisdom offers conflicting advice: "Shoot for the moon," but also, "Don't let the perfect be the enemy of the good." A new study by researchers at the University of Wyoming, Stanford University and the University of Colorado-Boulder used a mathematical model to

items.title: A hidden supermassive black hole may be lurking inside the Antennae galaxies
items.summary: Astronomers may have uncovered a hidden supermassive black hole inside the famous Antennae galaxies NGC 4038/4039, a pair of colliding galaxies best known for their spectacular bursts of star formation. The paper outlining the findings was posted to the arXiv preprint server on May 21.

items.title: Hubble Captures M88 on Journey to Center of Virgo Cluster
items.summary: This NASA Hubble Space Telescope image features the active spiral galaxy Messier 88 (M88), located about 63 million light-years away.

items.title: Homeless encampment sweeps spiked after Supreme Court decision
items.summary: Officials in Oakland sharply increased the number of homeless encampments they cleared in the months after the 2024 U.S. Supreme Court decision made it easier for municipalities nationwide to do so, new research from UC Berkeley shows.

items.title: Pandemic loan fraud pumped housing prices, research indicates
items.summary: For Americans dreaming of owning a home, this decade has been brutal. From the end of 2019 to the end of 2022, the median sales price for homes sold in the U.S. soared 35%, according to the Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis. It's dipped only slightly since then.

items.title: Topological states emerge in quantum Hall-superconductor devices with multiple channels
items.summary: Topological phases are unusual states of matter that give rise to properties protected by a material's overall structure (i.e., "topology"), as opposed to microscopic details. These phases are of great interest for the development of quantum technologies, as they can yield desirable electronic

items.title: Last-of-its-kind tree clinging to cliffside finds new hope at botanic gardens
items.summary: Conservationists are in a race against time to prevent one of the world's rarest island plants from disappearing forever, after seeds collected from the only surviving wild Dendroseris neriifolia tree arrived at the Millennium Seed Bank (MSB) at Kew Wakehurst in Sussex last month.

items.title: Five things to know about heat waves in Europe
items.summary: The scorching weather that has smashed temperature records across Europe this week shows the growing number and intensity of heat waves on the continent.

items.title: Flooding in north and east Syria as Euphrates level rises
items.summary: Syria's energy ministry warned on Thursday of rising water levels on the Euphrates River after flooding in the north and east following increased flows from neighboring Turkey and recent rains.

items.title: 'Atom Camera' maps laser light at nanoscale using a single ultracold atom
items.summary: A research group led by Assistant Professor Takafumi Tomita and Professor Kenji Ohmori at the Institute for Molecular Science, National Institutes of Natural Sciences, has developed a new microscopy technique called the Atom Camera, which uses a single ultracold atom at near absolute zero temperatur

items.title: Blue Origin rocket explodes on the launch pad during an engine-firing test
items.summary: A rocket belonging to Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin exploded during a test at the launch pad Thursday night, shaking nearby homes and briefly painting the sky orange.

items.title: What to know about Manhattanhenge, NYC's sunset spectacle
items.summary: New York City residents and visitors look up at the sky to experience a phenomenon twice a year known as Manhattanhenge.
